commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f9058855d336098b5e8f7a01932ed4280d378258)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a default-off `menuBarHidesCritters` setting, Display-pane toggle, and renderer/cache/signature plumbing so menu-bar meter icons can render as plain bars without provider decorations.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR. Current-main source confirms the exact plain-bar setting is absent, and the PR screenshots demonstrate the proposed after-state behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review is needed for the new persisted Display setting, and the contributor should fix the localized catalog blocker before normal merge review can pass.

target.title: feat: add "Hide critters" toggle to render plain menu-bar meter bars
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #1563 Add a **"Hide critters"** toggle in Settings → Display that renders every provider's menu-bar icon as plain capsule meter bars, dropping the face/decoration drawn inside the top bar (the Codex eyes + hat, Claude crab, Gemini/Antigravity sparkles, Factory gears, Warp eyes). Lets users who find the critter faces look like a glitch/obstruction keep the usage bars without switching to brand-icon-with-percent mode. ## Implementation - Added a persisted `menuBarHidesCritters` setting (default off) - Threaded through `SettingsStoreState.swift`, `SettingsStore.swift`, `SettingsStore+Defaults.swift`, and registered for menu observation in `SettingsStore+MenuObservation.swift` - Updated `Sources/CodexBar/IconRenderer.swift` - Added a `hideCritters` parameter to `makeIcon` and `makeMorphIcon` - Gated every decoration twist (`addFace` / `addNotches` / `addGeminiTwist` / `addAntigravityTwist` / `addFactoryTwist` / `addWarpTwist`) behind computed `twist*` booleans so the flag suppresses them while leaving the bar fills and tracks intact - Added `hideCritters` to both the icon cache key and the morph cache key so toggling doesn't return a stale render - Routed the flag through every live icon render path: `StatusItemController+Animation.swift` (merged, per-provider, and morph), `StatusItemController+Menu.swift`, and `MenuContent.swift` - Added `hideCritters` to the render-skip signatures in `+Animation.swift` and `+IconObservation.swift` so flipping the toggle repaints immediately instead of being deduped - Added the toggle row in `Sources/CodexBar/PreferencesDisplayPane.swift`, placed below the merge-icons rows and enabled only when "Merge icons" is off - Added `hide_critters_title` / `hide_critters_subtitle` strings to `en` plus the strict locales (`ar`, `fa`, `th`); remaining locales fall back to English at runtime ## Why The critter faces are cut into the meter bar itself (`IconRenderer` `addFace` et al.), so at menu-bar size the eye cutouts read as a stray mark obstructing the bars — reported by users as looking like a rendering glitch. The only existing escape was "Menu bar shows percent," which replaces the bars with a brand icon + text. This adds a way to keep the at-a-glance meter bars while removing just the decoration, consistently across all providers. ## Testing - Rendered all 52 `IconStyle` cases with `hideCritters: true` and confirmed each produces plain capsule bars (no faces, sparkles, dots, or eyes), including the intro morph crossfade - Ran `node Scripts/check-app-locales.mjs` — app locales OK (only pre-existing non-strict warnings) - Ran `swift build` — build complete ## Screenshots Before: After:
target.author: elijahfriedman
